---
title: "Defeating Nondeterminism in Your Writing Workflow"
author: "Your Name in collaboration with others"
date: "2026-06-20"
description: "A placeholder post demonstrating the blog's typography, citations, footnotes, and sidenotes."
---

Today's leading language models contain upwards of a trillion parameters,
pretrained on tens of trillions of tokens. The transformer architecture that
underpins them [@vaswani2017attention] keeps improving with scale, as these
trillions are necessary for learning and representing all the patterns in
written-down human knowledge.

::: {.column-margin}
This is a **margin note** — an unnumbered aside that lives in the right margin,
just like on the reference site. Use it for tangents, definitions, or asides.
:::

In contrast, post-training involves smaller datasets and generally focuses on
narrower domains of knowledge.[^post-training] This post is a placeholder that
demonstrates the typography and layout of the blog, including citations,
footnotes, and sidenotes.

[^post-training]: This sentence is anchored by a numbered footnote. Because the
    blog sets `reference-location: margin`, it renders as a sidenote in the
    margin rather than at the bottom of the page.

## A second-level heading

Body copy is set in Iowan Old Style at 17px with a generous line height, matching
the reference design. Links like [this one](https://example.com) pick up the
subtle underline treatment, and inline `code` is rendered in red monospace.

### An italic third-level heading

Lists use square bullets:

- First point about something interesting
- Second point with a bit more detail
- Third point to round things out

> Block quotes are styled with a thin left rule and a muted foreground color,
> exactly as on the reference site.

## Math and code

Inline math such as $e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0$ renders via KaTeX, and display math works too:

$$
\mathcal{L}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{x \sim \mathcal{D}}\big[ -\log p_\theta(x) \big]
$$

A fenced code block, here distilling a teacher into a student [@hinton2015distilling]:

```python
def distill(student, teacher, batch):
    logits_s = student(batch)
    logits_t = teacher(batch).detach()
    return kl_divergence(logits_s, logits_t)
```

## Closing thoughts

Replace this file with your real writing. New posts dropped into `posts/` show up
on the blogroll automatically. Add references to `references.bib` and cite them
with `[@key]`; footnotes (`[^id]`) and `::: {.column-margin}` blocks both surface
in the right margin.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
